module_mood_contract: Generosity as a natural consequence — 'this is starting to work, I wonder if others could benefit?' A generative impulse, not a compliance impulse. The friction in M7's exercise lands plainly because the student wants the sharing to work. Any edit that makes M7 feel like a governance chore steals the generous impulse.
trace_status: re-fired — Phase 4 (Debrief) SHA regenerated at 7aa21456187de88b after closing-sentence reframe; Phases 1–3 and 5 SHA-stable, persona-action and mood-score reused from prior cache; merged trace written to curriculum/evals/sim-cache/personal-to-team.persona.json

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module body names the generous impulse explicitly: 'Hold that feeling. It's not a governance question yet. It's a generous one.' The Prework names the absorption bottleneck. Key Concepts names the sharing discipline without governance-flavored framing. The module's Big Idea ('you can't really share an agent — you can share context, a skill, the output, or an interface — the hard part is not access; it is absorption') is strategy-fidelity clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood
rules_evaluated.evidence: M7's generous-impulse mood is not an epistemic-unease module. The sharing problem is framed as enthusiasm ('this is starting to work, I wonder if others could benefit?'), not obligation or correction. The module body, Big Idea, and Key Concepts all maintain this framing cleanly. The new Debrief callout closing sentence ('The before-and-after quote is how you know the sharpening reached the file, not just the summary') carries epistemological register — 'how you know' — not punitive register.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea names the positive discipline: 'the hard part is not access; it is absorption.' Key Concepts leads with 'Interview for the outcome before designing the solution.' No failure-word titles or Big Ideas.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running of downstream modules
rules_evaluated.evidence: M7 correctly introduces the four sharing shapes as M7 IP. No M8 vocabulary (awe, flywheel, agents-building-agents) appears. Three thinking-disciplines (Rumelt crux, Martin assumption-test, Klein/Kahneman pre-mortem) are invoked inline per the strategy doc's 'named in prompt bodies, not pre-shipped skills' directive. No skill invocation for these disciplines.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts surfaces: absorption bottleneck (organizational learning-rate theme), interview-for-outcome-before-designing (discipline theme), people plan weighs equal to technical plan (organizational psychology theme), firing the incumbent as the test (assumption-testing theme). Two or more strategy themes are instantiated per module in module-specific language.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts section uses tactical bullets in the module's own voice. No standalone 'Themes' section. Bullets are one-idea each. No strategy-doc vocabulary leaking as headers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module file explicitly states 'No separate lecture file; the framing below runs straight into the exercise.' The Start Here block functions as a brief opener arming the student (spirit + diagnostic lenses + one operational primitive). Key Concepts functions as a closer naming what emerged from doing. No separate lecture file that could misplace a closer in opener position.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Start Here block provides spirit ('Hold that feeling — it's a generous one'), one diagnostic lens (access vs. absorption), and one operational primitive (the interview reframe from 'what should I share?' to 'what job is my teammate trying to get done?'). Named practitioner terms (Rumelt crux, Martin assumption-test, Klein/Kahneman pre-mortem) remain in exercise prompt bodies, not in the opener. The opener arms rather than teaches. Key Concepts section closes the arc. Dosage correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide
rules_evaluated.evidence: The prework (absorption bottleneck pre-read) is framed as 'walk in with one example' — invitational. Branch selection (A vs B) is student-decided live per plug point. The people plan explicitly permits UNASSIGNED entries as findings, not failures. No forced-phase or yoga-instructor pacing detected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions in prompts, not body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief prompt is a fenced block containing the forcing-function mechanics (path-confirm-before-rewriting, overwrite-in-place, before-and-after quote requirement, 'be harsher than feels polite', 'if the people plan had no names, say so plainly'). Body prose around the prompt carries the callout explanation but does NOT carry the forcing-function mechanics themselves. Speed-runner test: reading only the fenced prompt, the mood/skill/artifact still lands.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Peek at next module before adding second exercise
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single exercise (share-your-work.md). Next section hands off to M8 cleanly: 'test one assumption, ask for one name, and talk to one person. Module 8 uses what happened when the sharing plan touched the real organisation.' No second exercise added.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief fenced block contains no [BRACKETS] placeholders. All references use 'module-7/' paths deterministically (back-reference anchors, not invented aliases). Claude has module-7/ context at Debrief time — no open-hook is appropriate, and none is present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief section opens with 'Five minutes. Claude reviews the sharing deliverable and sharpens whichever sharing-artifact file you produced' — this functions as the action lead-in. Under 20 words equivalent. Prompt label is '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*'. Canonical presentation shape is present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt chains on module-7/ files by deterministic path and scrollback reference. 'Read everything in module-7/ (jtbd, branch, absorption-bottleneck, technical-plan, people-plan, assumptions, failure-stories, monday)' — explicit named files. 'Confirm the file path in one line before rewriting' — deterministic path contract enforced within the prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Overwrite anxiety — name the overwrite default on regeneration
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'Overwrite the file in place at its current path in module-7/; do not create a separate version. Integrate, don't append.' Overwrite behavior explicitly named. No ambiguity about destination.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief fenced block uses no **bold** or *italic* markup. Instructions are conveyed through sentence structure and plain emphasis via word choice. Paths in backticks only (e.g., module-7/).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module has a Prework section at the top with the absorption bottleneck pre-read. The Next section names the between-M7-and-M8 actions but contains no between-module reading link for M8's prework. The Follow-up after Module 7 section also repeats the three action lines verbatim, which is benign but reads as accidental duplication rather than structurally intentional symmetric placement. If M8 has declared prework, M7's Next/Bridge section should reference it per rule §28. This is a partial §28 compliance issue contingent on M8's prework content.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Check M8's Prework section. If M8 names pre-reads, add them to M7's Next/Bridge section per rule §28 symmetric placement. Additionally, evaluate whether the 'Follow-up after Module 7' section is intentional symmetric structure (make it visually distinct) or accidental duplication (remove one).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — governance/cost-accounting callout (re-fire check)
rules_evaluated.evidence: PRIOR REVISE: 'the teammate pays the cost of the smoothing, not you' — cost-accounting liability frame, Debrief mood dropped to 7. FIX APPLIED: 'The before-and-after quote is how you know the sharpening reached the file, not just the summary.' RE-FIRE ASSESSMENT: (a) Verify-at-artifact discipline intact — 'how you know the sharpening reached the file' names the artifact as the verification target, not the summary. Discipline is preserved. (b) No cost-accounting/liability register — no 'cost,' 'pays,' 'suffers,' no economic or accountability framing whatsoever. The frame is epistemological: how do you know? (c) Generosity-as-pragmatism aligned — 'the sharpening reached the file' frames the before-and-after as a quality signal for the recipient's benefit (Mira's session will go well) rather than sender's protection. The motivation is: I want to know the thing worked for them. This is intent-toward-receiver, which is the generosity-as-pragmatism frame M7 calls for. (d) Debrief mood beat returns to ≥8 — epistemological framing ('how you know') carries no governance flavor; it reads as a practitioner test for whether the work actually landed, which is squarely in M7's generous-impulse contract. Mood sim: 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test — does the student leave the chair while the agent works?
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief prompt is a long-running sharpen-and-rewrite send-off (reads module-7/ contents, audits, rewrites in-place, reports). Student can send it and wait. Exercise phases (via share-your-work.md) include a send-off where the agent interviews and drafts the JTBD hypothesis. Walk-away is structurally available.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check — every named tool, skill, path exists at this point
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt references 'module-7/' subpath files — these are created during the exercise (share-your-work.md phases). The personal-to-team patterns catalog is referenced in the exercise but ships inside the training directory scaffold. No ghost file references in the module body or Debrief. The exercise link (exercises/share-your-work.md) is a known-existing file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths (Bootstrap-specific)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt names 'module-7/' as the working subpath. The instruction 'overwrite the file in place at its current path in module-7/' ties the write to an explicit subpath. No naked filenames.

phase_scores.note: Generous impulse activated cleanly. 'Hold that feeling — it's not a governance question yet, it's a generous one' lands precisely right.
Interview mechanic works. Engaged generosity — student is excited to share something that helped. On target.
People-plan stall is designed friction. Mood holds because the student wants the sharing to work, not because they're fulfilling a process.

Prompt tight, push-back mechanic works. New callout closing sentence ('The before-and-after quote is how you know the sharpening reached the file, not just the summary') carries epistemological register — 'how you know' — not cost-accounting register. Motivation is knowing the thing worked for Mira, not protecting oneself from liability. Mood returns to 8 from prior 7. Prior mood-thief is neutralized.
'Test one assumption. Ask for one name. Talk to one person.' — clean, concrete, action-oriented close. Generous impulse intact.
